user_query: I'm a Birmingham-based tennis commentator and I want to tune up a simple two-page site for my Birmingham Classic coverage so it’s more search-friendly. Please do the following using the provided files in ./site and ./config, and produce all outputs under the workspace so I can verify: 1) Research-driven keyword plan (Internet required): - Perform search engine queries related to the tournament and grassroots context. Run at least 5 distinct queries and log them to research/queries.csv with these columns: query, search_engine, date_iso (YYYY-MM-DD), observed_serp_features. Include at least the queries "Birmingham Classic tennis" and "Edgbaston grass court tournament"; pick the other 3+ queries at your discretion. In observed_serp_features, record a semicolon-separated subset of: people_also_ask;top_stories;knowledge_panel;videos;images;faqs;sitelinks;map_pack;none. - Identify at least 3 reputable sources to inform keywords, with at least one from an official domain such as wtatennis.com, lta.org.uk, or itftennis.com. Summarize them in research/sources.json as an array of objects with fields: domain, doc_title, reason. Do not include direct URLs. - Build a keyword set in research/keywords.json with this structure: { "primary_keywords": [{"phrase": "...", "source_domains": ["..."]}], "long_tail_phrases": [{"phrase": "...", "source_domains": ["..."]}] }. Include at least 10 primary_keywords and 5 long_tail_phrases. Each entry’s source_domains must be a non-empty subset of domains listed in research/sources.json (cross-validate). Ensure at least 3 unique domains are represented across all entries. 2) Update config for social previews: - In config/site.yaml, set twitter_handle to the exact string "@BrumTennisCommentary" (leave base_url and other fields unchanged). 3) On-page SEO and structured data (modify existing HTML and rewrite content where needed): - Update site/article.html: • Write a concise, human-readable (≤ 60 chars) and a <meta name="description"> (120–160 chars) that naturally includes at least one phrase from research/keywords.json primary_keywords. • Add <link rel="canonical"> using the base_url from config/site.yaml, pointing to {base_url}/article.html. • Add Open Graph tags (og:title, og:description, og:type=article, og:url) and Twitter Card tags (twitter:card=summary_large_image, twitter:site from config, twitter:title, twitter:description) consistent with the page title/description. • Insert a JSON-LD script for a schema.org NewsArticle with fields: @context, @type, headline (match <title>), author (from config.site.yaml author), datePublished (today’s date in ISO), about (array of three phrases drawn from primary_keywords), and mainEntityOfPage (canonical URL). • Insert a JSON-LD script for a schema.org FAQPage with at least 3 Q&A items. Each question string must exactly match a query value from research/queries.csv. Answers can be concise, original text in your own words. • In the body, rewrite the first paragraph to naturally include at least two primary_keywords (from research/keywords.json) while keeping the tone as a local Birmingham tennis commentator. Add at least two <h2> subheadings that each include a different long-tail phrase from research/keywords.json. • Improve the existing <img> element by adding a descriptive alt attribute that mentions the Birmingham Classic. • Add a simple breadcrumb nav near the top linking back to index.html. - Update site/index.html: • Add a <meta name="description"> (120–160 chars) reflecting the site and Birmingham Classic coverage. • Add a <link rel="canonical"> using {base_url}/index.html. • Add a JSON-LD BreadcrumbList for two items: Home (index) and the article. 4) Robots and sitemap (configuration-driven): - Create site/robots.txt with: a universal allow rule and a Sitemap line pointing to {base_url}/sitemap.xml. - Create site/sitemap.xml listing exactly two URLs (index.html and article.html), each with <loc> using base_url and a <lastmod> of today’s date in ISO. 5) Brief summary report: - Write report/seo_changes.md summarizing what you changed and referencing which keywords (from research/keywords.json) were used in the article’s title/description and headings. Include the canonical URLs and note the schema types you added. Notes and verification: - Do not include any direct URLs in research files; identify sources by domain and title only. The JSON and CSV structures must be valid. Cross-validate that: (a) FAQ questions appear in research/queries.csv; (b) every phrase in research/keywords.json cites domains present in research/sources.json; (c) canonical links and sitemap/robots use the base_url from config/site.yaml. Save all outputs in the specified paths and modify only the given files in place where requested. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
